questions.prompt: Who usually overrides the roadmap?
questions.framing: When plans change, who actually decides?

options.label: CEO / exec team
options.description: Roadmap and priorities shift to the top of the house.

options.label: Committee or leadership group
options.description: Alignment rituals replace local team judgment.

options.label: Teams mostly decide
options.description: Teams keep deciding until pressure forces escalation.

questions.prompt: How often do top priorities shift?
questions.framing: Help us stress-test your philosophy against your cadence.

options.label: Weekly or more
options.description: Plans change faster than teams can absorb.

options.label: Monthly
options.description: Quarterly goals rarely survive the month.

options.label: Quarterly or slower
options.description: Direction holds until an exception breaks it.

questions.prompt: What happens when revenue or growth misses?
questions.framing: Under pressure, orgs behave differently than their values say.

options.label: Decisions recentralize
options.description: Control moves upward to manage perceived risk.

options.label: Experiments and bets get cut
options.description: Innovation is sidelined for immediate survival.

options.label: Headcount reductions
options.description: Cost-cutting measures focus on talent overhead.

options.label: Double down on current bets
options.description: Rigidity increases as current paths are prioritized.

questions.prompt: Where does work most often stall?
questions.framing: Where is the binding constraint today?

options.label: Design approval
options.description: Shipping waits on taste and approval cycles.

options.label: Engineering capacity
options.description: Backlog depth sets the real clock speed.

options.label: Exec review / calendar
options.description: Calendar density becomes the bottleneck.

options.label: Compliance / legal / security
options.description: Risk review slows anything customer-visible.

options.label: No clear bottleneck
options.description: Work stalls in different places each sprint.

questions.prompt: What's your org's AI reality today?
questions.framing: AI changes which philosophies actually work.

options.label: AI-first mandate from leadership
options.description: Mandates outpace workflow redesign on the ground.

options.label: Pilots and pockets of adoption
options.description: Pockets adopt; the core operating model stays human-paced.

options.label: Engineering-led AI shipping
options.description: Engineering ships AI features ahead of org-wide adoption.

options.label: Skeptical or slow adoption
options.description: Caution slows investment in production AI paths.

options.label: Heavy review — design, eng, exec sign-off

questions.prompt: How many people truly own outcomes end-to-end (barrels)?
